[[General/Legends|General]] '''Herron Dade''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gandle Ott]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. By [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Dade was the highest [[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] native in the planet's [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-controlled government, and he [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] the [[Gandle Ott planetary militia|planetary militia]]. Following the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|defeat]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]—[[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] ruler of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]]—at [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Sarne retreated to Gandle Ott with his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. There, he stripped the planet of its [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]] and [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|Governor]] before departing. Gandle Ott was thrown into chaos, and the former civilian government, the [[Bank parliament]], stepped in to restore order. Dade emerged as one of the leading candidates to take control of the planet, the other being [[Marja Lang]], the Imperial [[Lieutenant governor/Legends|Vice-Governor]] who had been left behind during Sarne's withdrawal. Dade was kept busy consolidating the remaining security and defense forces, and worried about Gandle Ott's ability to defend itself. When the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived at Gandle Ott in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of Sarne, Dade attempted to gain their support as Gandle Ott's "native son," hoping that their backing, combined with his own popularity among the civilian population, would enable him to ascend to the position of [[President/Legends|President]] of the Bank parliament.

==Biography==
Herron Dade was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on [[Gandle Ott]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. When Gandle Ott was under the control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="DIRP" />—and later, under [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] after he withdrew the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] from the Empire following [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="tdc">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref>—Dade was the highest-[[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] native in government, serving as [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Gandle Ott planetary militia|planetary militia]] with the rank of [[General/Legends|General]]. Although he served the Imperial government, he had no love for the Empire but never made his feelings public.<ref name="DIRP" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Moff Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Sarne retreated with the majority of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] to Gandle Ott, where he claimed to be organizing a massive [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to purge the sector of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. He made no mention of his defeat at Kal'Shebbol and began uplifting supplies from [[Crimler Naval Base]]. After Sarne's departure, the population of Gandle Ott discovered that he had transferred the majority of the Imperial personnel stationed on the planet to his fleet—including the [[Unidentified Imperial Governor (Gandle Ott)|Governor]]—and left with most of the [[Gandle Ott system defense force|defense force]].<ref name="DIRP" />

With the loss of Imperial command authority on the planet, the [[Bank parliament]]—the civilian authority that had been relegated to an advisory capacity under Imperial control—re-established control over Gandle Ott. Sarne had left the Imperial [[Lieutenant governor/Legends|Vice-Governor]], [[Marja Lang]], behind due to her fervent loyalty to the Empire over Sarne, and she began to consolidate her power base among her fellow abandoned Imperials. Dade, relieved that Sarne had gone and determined that Gandle Ott would not fall under outside control again, began to gather his own power base and worked to displace Lang as the Acting-[[Governor/Legends|Governor]]. His plan was to re-establish the dormant position of [[President/Legends|President]] of the Bank parliament, which had been abolished when the Empire had assumed control of Gandle Ott, and maneuver himself to be the prime candidate. However, Dade was extremely worried that the planet no longer had the means to defend itself from the pirates that plagued the sector.<ref name="DIRP" />

[[File:Mushroom_Cutting_ceremony.jpg|thumb|left|300px|General Dade presides over the Mushroom Cutting ceremony, watched by the media and members of the ''FarStar'' crew.]]
Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after Sarne's departure, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in [[DarkStryder Campaign|pursuit]] of the rogue Moff. Due to work commitments, Dade was unable to join the governmental delegation, led by Acting-Governor Lang, that met the ''FarStar'' crew at [[Montrol Spaceport]]. While Dade had no intention of letting outside forces once again control Gandle Ott, he welcomed the presence of the New Republic and hoped to gain their endorsement as Gandle Ott's "native son." The following day, despite his hectic schedule, Dade presided over the [[Ceremonial Mushroom Cutting]] that he used to symbolically declare Gandle Ott's independence from the Empire. He met the New Republic delegation for the first [[Time/Legends|time]] and used their presence at the ceremony to legitimize his stance. Dade spoke of the need for Gandle Ott to take control of its own affairs in the wake of the Imperial departure, and he was the center of attention for the [[Media/Legends|media]] during the event while Lang, who had been maneuvered into the attending the ceremony by the General, was bored throughout the proceedings.<ref name="DIRP" />

During the following days, Dade was kept busy consolidating the military and security forces still on the planet, restricting his availability to meet with the ''FarStar'' delegation to the evenings. Through information culled from [[CUTH-BRT-92-X3]], the [[BRT supercomputer/Legends|BRT supercomputer]] that controlled Gandle Ott's capital [[City/Legends|settlement]], [[Montrol City]], he learned that the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]] of the ''[[FarStar]]'', [[Gorak Khzam]],<ref name="DIRP" /> was a former member of the [[Sabiador Slavers]]. As Khzam had kept his past a secret from his crewmates,<ref name="tdc" /> Dade was able to use that leverage over the Rodian to influence him into encouraging the rest of the ''FarStar'' delegation to support the General. On the evening of the second day of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} visit, Dade hosted a private dinner for the New Republic delegation and another ninety-five guests at his [[Herron Dade's estates|private estates]]. On the fourth day of their visit, Dade and other military officials met with the ''FarStar'' delegation to discuss potential military aid that the New Republic could provide to Gandle Ott. After several days on the planet, during which [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] relations were established between Gandle Ott and the New Republic, the ''FarStar'' departed to continue its mission.<ref name="DIRP" />

==Personality and traits==
Herron Dade was a thin, gaunt Human with iron-[[gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and mustache who chain-smoked [[Chak-root/Legends|chak-root]]. A military man, Dade was not particularly subtle or versed in the art of diplomacy. He would say something inappropriate or awkward at times, but he was popular with the citizens of the planet. Dade believed he could use his popularity to ascend to the position of President of Gandle Ott. Like many of his fellow natives, Dade had no love for Sarne or the Empire but kept that fact to himself. Following the departure of the Empire, Dade was welcoming of the New Republic, but was adamant that Gandle Ott should be able to rule itself and saw Lang and her Imperial faction as a threat to that goal. Dade had a talent for reading people, and he had access to vast amounts of data gathered by CUTH-BRT-92-X3.<ref name="DIRP" />

As head of the planetary militia, Dade was trained in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and could operate vehicle-mounted weapons. He was also versed in [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]] tactics, and had experience with explosives and security systems. Dade had an understanding of the workings of a [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]], was knowledgeable about law enforcement procedures and tactics, and specialized in investigations based on Gandle Ott. He was also well informed about the [[Ott system]] in general. He regularly carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and a ceremonial [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]].<ref name="DIRP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Herron Dade was introduced in ''[[Death Is Remotely Possible]]'', the third [[Roleplaying game|RPG]] adventure in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. During the course of the adventure, the player characters have to deal with CUTH-BRT-92-X3's attempts to delay the ''FarStar'' from pursuing Sarne, interference that manifests in several ways, including near fatal traffic accidents and an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] being unleashed upon them. Dade, along with Lang, is one of the officials to whom the players can report these occurrences, along with the suspicion that CUTH-BRT-92-X3 is responsible. Dade takes the information seriously and looks into the matter, which leads to his discovery of protocol modifications made to the unit by Moff Sarne. CUTH-BRT-92-X3 is taken offline and [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] are employed to restore the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to normal. As the player characters can report to either Dade or Lang, this article makes no assumption as to which official investigates the claims of CUTH-BRT-92-X3's aberrant behavior.
